text: In Agile methodology, teams work in short cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two weeks, and developers focus on completing specific tasks from the project backlog. The backlog is a list of features and fixes, often described as user stories—simple explanations of what the user needs. Every morning, the team holds a brief standup meeting where they discuss progress and any obstacles. This approach is called iterative development because the product is built step by step, improving with each sprint. By reviewing and adjusting regularly, Agile teams can respond to changes quickly and deliver value faster.
